data.body:
PASS 1 review (criterion T1 — Prior-art / novel-architecture justification [overlay]): no findings.

T1 is an OVERLAY that applies only when a bright-line fires. None does here: this Cluster-2 plan is a pure in-file refactor that collapses two structurally identical jittered-exponential-backoff functions (`_compute_backoff_seconds`, `_cas_backoff_seconds`) into one parameterized pure helper `_jittered_backoff(...)`. There is no external integration, no new/unfamiliar dependency (it stays on stdlib `random.uniform`, already imported at `_advisory_lock.py:19`), no security/auth surface, no performance/scalability target, and no data/schema migration. Jittered exponential backoff (`min(base*factor**i, cap) * uniform(1±jitter)`) is a well-trodden pattern, so no prior-art search is warranted (T1 ANTI-FP).

Affirmations for T1:
- (a) Prior art / no reinvention: the plan is explicitly anti-reinvention — it consolidates existing duplicated math rather than inventing a new mechanism, and the helper is a thin parameterization of the formula already present in both functions.
- (b) Novelty justified vs established approach (anti-repackaging / Rule-of-Three): the plan affirmatively applies this discipline — AC line "No universal retry() helper or new _retry.py module is introduced; the change stays inside _advisory_lock.py", and the What section notes "the other retry sites solve different problems and are intentionally left bespoke (see parent epic civil-marlin-flare)". This is the correct Rule-of-Three scoping (unify only the one genuine duplication, do not over-abstract).
- (c) Unverified capability assertions: none present; the only library call asserted (`random.uniform`, one draw, preserving RNG draw count/order) is verified against the existing code at `_advisory_lock.py:136` and `:428`, and AC line 3 pins the existing jitter test to guard the RNG-sequence claim.

Clean chunk for T1.
